expected_behavior: 1 entity x 1 metric x 3 years = 3 sub-questions. Multi-period fan-out retrieval. Aggregator with YoY growth.
question: Show Microsoft Intelligent Cloud segment revenue FY2022 to FY2024 with year-over-year growth rates.
ground_truth: Microsoft Intelligent Cloud segment revenue: FY2022 ~$75.3B, FY2023 ~$87.9B (+17% YoY), FY2024 ~$105.4B (+20% YoY). Azure growth was a primary driver each year. Each figure should cite the respective 10-K filing.
key_facts: Must cite [SEC-MSFT-*] chunks for each year.

pipeline.answer:
# Microsoft Intelligent Cloud Segment Revenue: FY2022–FY2024

## Overview

Microsoft's Intelligent Cloud segment (comprising Azure, enterprise services, and other cloud infrastructure) has demonstrated accelerating growth over the past three fiscal years, driven primarily by Azure and AI services adoption.

## Annual Revenue and Growth Rates

| Fiscal Year | Intelligent Cloud Revenue | Year-over-Year Growth |
|-------------|--------------------------|----------------------|
| **FY2022**  | **$74,965 million** ($75.0B) | — (base year) |
| **FY2023**  | **$87,907 million** ($87.9B) | **+17%** |
| **FY2024**  | **$105,362 million** ($105.4B) | **+20%** |

*Data sourced from Microsoft's official annual reports [3][4][6]*

## Growth Trajectory Analysis

### FY2022→FY2023: 17% Growth
- Revenue increased from $74,965M to $87,907M [3][4]
- Primary driver: Azure and other cloud services growth [3]
- Reflects continued enterprise cloud migration and adoption [2]

### FY2023→FY2024: 20% Growth
- Revenue increased from $87,907M to $105,362M [3][6]
- Growth acceleration from 17% to 20%, marking the highest rate in the three-year period [1][3]
- Key driver: Azure, with Microsoft explicitly noting the increase was "driven by Azure" [5]
- AI services and OpenAI workloads began contributing meaningfully to revenue during this period [1][5]

## Segment Comparison (FY2022–FY2024)

By FY2024, Intelligent Cloud became Microsoft's **largest revenue segment**, surpassing both Productivity & Business Processes and More Personal Computing:

| Segment | FY2022 | FY2023 | FY2024 |
|---------|--------|--------|--------|
| Productivity & Business Processes | $63,364M | $69,274M | $77,728M |
| **Intelligent Cloud** | **$74,965M** | **$87,907M** | **$105,362M** |
| More Personal Computing | $59,941M | $54,734M | $62,032M |
| **Total Revenue** | **$198,270M** | **$211,915M** | **$245,122M** |

*All figures in millions of U.S. dollars, per Microsoft's fiscal years ending June 30 [3][4]*

## Growth Drivers

1. **Azure:** The primary growth engine across all three years, with consistent double-digit expansion [3][5]
2. **AI Services:** By late FY2024/Q1 FY2025, Microsoft disclosed its AI business reached a $37 billion annual revenue run rate, accelerating growth [1][5]
3. **Enterprise Cloud Adoption:** Ongoing digital transformation across industries fueled steady demand for cloud infrastructure and services [2]

## Post-FY2024 Trajectory (Context)

The acceleration trend continued beyond FY2024, with the Intelligent Cloud segment posting **30% year-over-year growth** in subsequent quarterly results [1][5], indicating sustained momentum from AI and cloud investments.

---

## Key Takeaways

| Metric | FY2022 | FY2023 | FY2024 |
|--------|--------|--------|--------|
| Revenue ($B) | $75.0B | $87.9B | $105.4B |
| YoY Growth | — | **+17%** | **+20%** |
| Growth Trend | — | Steady | **Accelerating** |
| Primary Driver | Azure | Azure | Azure + AI |

- **Intelligent Cloud grew from $75B to $105.4B over three years** — a $30.4B absolute increase
- **Growth rate accelerated from 17% to 20%**, driven by Azure and the ramp-up of AI services
- The segment **became Microsoft's largest revenue contributor** by FY2024, surpassing all other segments
- **AI emerged as a key growth catalyst** by FY2024, with the trend continuing into subsequent fiscal years [1][5]

urls.title: Microsoft 2023 Annual Report
urls.snippet: # Microsoft Annual Report 2023. We are living through a time of historic challenge and opportunity. At the same time, we have entered a new age of AI that will fundamentally transform productivity for every individual, organization, and industry on earth, and help us address some of our most pressing challenges. This next generation of AI will reshape every software category and every business, including our own. As a company, we believe we can be the democratizing force for this new generation of technology and the opportunity it will help unlock for every country, community, and individual, while mitigating its risks. To build on this progress, we remain convicted on three things: First, we will maintain our lead as the top commercial cloud while innovating in consumer categories, from gaming to professional social networks. As we make progress on these priorities, we delivered strong results in fiscal year 2023, including a record $211 billion in revenue and over $88 billion in operating income.

urls.title: FY24 Q3 - Intelligent Cloud Performance - Investor Relations - Microsoft
urls.snippet: •  Server products and cloud services revenue increased $4.8 billion or 24% driven by Azure and other cloud services. Azure and other cloud services revenue grew 31% driven by growth in our consumption-based services. •  Gross margin increased $3.1 billion or 20% driven by growth in Azure. Excluding the impact of the change in accounting estimate, gross margin percentage increased slightly primarily driven by improvement in Azure, inclusive of scaling our AI infrastructure, offset in part by sales mix shift to Azure. *IMPORTANT NOTICE TO USERS (summary only, click here  for full text of notice);*All information is unaudited unless otherwise noted or accompanied by an audit opinion and is subject to the more comprehensive information contained in our SEC reports and filings. All information speaks as of the last fiscal quarter or year for which we have filed a Form 10-K or 10-Q, or for historical information the date or period expressly indicated in or with such information. Information contained in these documents is current as of the earnings date, and not restated for new accounting standards.

urls.title: FY24 Q4 - Intelligent Cloud Performance - Investor Relations - Microsoft
urls.snippet: •  Server products and cloud services revenue increased $17.8 billion or 22% driven by Azure and other cloud services. •  Gross margin increased $11.6 billion or 19% driven by growth in Azure. Excluding the impact of the change in accounting estimate, gross margin percentage increased slightly primarily driven by improvement in Azure, inclusive of scaling our AI infrastructure, offset in part by sales mix shift to Azure. •  Operating expenses decreased slightly primarily driven by the prior year Q2 charge, offset in part by investments in Azure. All information speaks as of the last fiscal quarter or year for which we have filed a Form 10-K or 10-Q, or for historical information the date or period expressly indicated in or with such information. Information contained in these documents is current as of the earnings date, and not restated for new accounting standards. #### Related Information.

urls.title: Microsoft 2024 Annual Report
urls.snippet: *   [Income Taxes](https://www.microsoft.com/investor/reports/ar24/index.html#income-taxes). This year, we also announced a [partnership with G42](https://news.microsoft.com/2024/04/15/microsoft-invests-1-5-billion-in-abu-dhabis-g42-to-accelerate-ai-development-and-global-expansion/), which will run its AI applications and services on our cloud, as we collaborate to bring our latest AI technologies to the United Arab Emirates and other countries. Our [Microsoft Intelligent Data Platform](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-cloud/solutions/intelligent-data-platform) provides customers with the broadest capabilities spanning databases, analytics, business intelligence, and governance—along with seamless integration with all our AI services. And [Microsoft Fabric](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-fabric), our AI-powered, next-generation data platform we made generally available this year, now has over 14,000 paid customers who can go from data, to insights, to action—all within the same unified SaaS solution. The decrease from the federal statutory rate in fiscal year 2022 is primarily due to the net income tax benefit related to the transfer of intangible properties, earnings taxed at lower rates in foreign jurisdictions resulting from producing and distributing our products and services through our foreign regional operations center in Ireland, and tax benefits relating to stock-based compensation.

urls.title: MLQ.ai | AI for investors
urls.snippet: * Microsoft's AI business reached a $37 billion annual revenue run rate, up 123% year-over-year, marking the first update since reporting $13 billion in January 2025[1]. * Azure and cloud services revenue grew 40% in the quarter, accelerating past the company's own forecast and topping analyst expectations[1]. * Microsoft Cloud revenue hit $54.5 billion with 29% growth, while the Intelligent Cloud segment generated $34.7 billion with 30% growth[1]. The Intelligent Cloud segment, home to Azure, posted $34.7 billion in revenue with 30% year-over-year growth, while the company disclosed that its AI business has reached a $37 billion annual revenue run rate, up 123% from a year ago[1]. The company's AI business surpassed the $37 billion annual revenue run rate milestone, up 123% year-over-year and nearly triple the $13 billion run rate disclosed in January 2025[1][3]. Capital expenditures decreased to $31.9 billion from $37.5 billion in the previous quarter, but the company clarified that this decline reflected the timing of data center construction and hardware deliveries rather than reduced demand for cloud and AI services[1].

urls.title: FY24 Q4 - Performance - Investor Relations - Microsoft
urls.snippet: Revenue increased $33.2 billion or 16% driven by growth across each of our segments. Cost of revenue increased $8.3 billion or 13% driven by growth in Microsoft Cloud and Gaming, offset in part by a decline in Devices. Gross margin increased $25.0 billion or 17% driven by growth across each of our segments. Excluding the impact of the change in accounting estimate for the useful lives of our server and network equipment, gross margin percentage increased 2 points driven by improvement in More Personal Computing. Excluding the impact of the change in accounting estimate, Microsoft Cloud gross margin percentage increased slightly driven by improvements in Azure and Office 365 Commercial, inclusive of scaling our AI infrastructure, offset in part by sales mix shift to Azure. Operating expenses increased $4.0 billion or 7% driven by Gaming, with 7 points of growth from the Activision Blizzard acquisition, and investments in cloud engineering, offset in part by the prior year Q2 charge.

chunks.title: MLQ.ai | AI for investors
chunks.chunk_text: Microsoft reported third-quarter earnings that exceeded Wall Street expectations across all major metrics, with the company's cloud and AI businesses driving substantial revenue growth. The Intelligent Cloud segment, home to Azure, posted $34.7 billion in revenue with 30% year-over-year growth, while the company disclosed that its AI business has reached a $37 billion annual revenue run rate, up 123% from a year ago[1](https://mlq.ai/news/microsoft-cloud-and-ai-acceleration-drive-market-outperformance-despite-capital-intensity-concerns/# "Click to view source").

chunks.title: Infographic: Cloud Business Continues to Drive Microsoft's Growth
chunks.heading: Cloud Business Continues to Drive Microsoft's Growth
chunks.chunk_text:
Cloud Business Continues to Drive Microsoft's Growth

Microsoft's Intelligent Cloud segment alone generates more than $100 billion in annual revenue now, as global demand for cloud infrastructure continues to grow. The transition to AI has given another boost to the [global cloud market](https://www.statista.com/chart/34022/cloud-infrastructure-service-revenues/), re-accelerating a market that has grown from less than $100 billion in 2019 to more than $400 billion in 2025. According to estimates from [Synergy Research](https://www.statista.com/chart/18819/worldwide-market-share-of-leading-cloud-infrastructure-service-providers/), Microsoft is one of the market leaders in the cloud infrastructure market, trailing only Amazon Web Services (AWS) in terms of market share.

![Image 15](https://cdn.statcdn.com/contactPerson/367.jpg)

Felix Richter

Data Journalist

[felix.richter@statista.com](mailto:felix.richter@statista.com) +49 (40) 284 841 557

![Image 16: Infographic: Cloud Business Continues to Drive Microsoft's Growth | Statista](https://cdn.statcdn.com/Infographic/images/normal/29863.jpeg)

Description

This chart shows Microsoft's cloud revenue and total revenue per fiscal year.

[Report](https://www.statista.com/statistics/report-content/infographic/29863 "Report")

[Download Chart](https://cdn.statcdn.com/Infographic/images/normal/29863.jpeg "Download Chart")

chunks.title: Microsoft 2024 Annual Report
chunks.heading: SEGMENT RESULTS OF OPERATIONS
chunks.chunk_text:
SEGMENT RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

**(In millions, except percentages)****2024****2023****Percentage

Change**
**Revenue**
Productivity and Business Processes**$ 77,728**$ 69,274 12%
Intelligent Cloud**105,362**87,907 20%
More Personal Computing**62,032**54,734 13%
Total**$ 245,122**$ 211,915 16%
**Operating Income**
Productivity and Business Processes**$ 40,540**$ 34,189 19%
Intelligent Cloud**49,584**37,884 31%
More Personal Computing**19,309**16,450 17%
Total**$ 109,433**$ 88,523 24%

**Reportable Segments**

**_Fiscal Year 2024 Compared with Fiscal Year 2023_**

_Productivity and Business Processes_

Revenue increased $8.5 billion or 12%.

*   Office Commercial products and cloud services revenue increased $5.8 billion or 14%. Office 365 Commercial revenue grew 16% with seat growth of 7%, driven by small and medium business and frontline worker offerings, as well as growth in revenue per user. Office Commercial products revenue declined 16% driven by continued customer shift to cloud offerings.
*   Office Consumer products and cloud services revenue increased $237 million or 4%. Microsoft 365 Consumer subscribers grew 10% to 82.5 million.
*   LinkedIn revenue increased $1.4 billion or 9% driven by growth across all lines of business – Talent Solutions, Premium Subscriptions, Marketing Solutions, and Sales Solutions.
*   Dynamics products and cloud services revenue increased $1.0 billion or 19% driven by Dynamics 365. Dynamics 365 revenue grew 24% driven by growth across all workloads.

Total**$ 88,523**$ 83,383 6%

**Reportable Segments**

**_Fiscal Year 2023 Compared with Fiscal Year 2022_**

_Productivity and Business Processes_

Revenue increased $5.9 billion or 9%.

*   Office Commercial products and cloud services revenue increased $3.7 billion or 10%. Office 365 Commercial revenue grew 13% with seat growth of 11%, driven by small and medium business and frontline worker offerings, as well as growth in revenue per user. Office Commercial products revenue declined 21% driven by continued customer shift to cloud offerings.
*   Office Consumer products and cloud services revenue increased $140 million or 2%. Microsoft 365 Consumer subscribers grew 12% to 67.0 million.
*   LinkedIn revenue increased $1.3 billion or 10% driven by Talent Solutions.
*   Dynamics products and cloud services revenue increased $750 million or 16% driven by Dynamics 365 growth of 24%.

Operating income increased $4.5 billion or 15%.

chunks.title: Microsoft tops Wall Street expectations, reports accelerating Azure growth and $37B AI run rate
chunks.heading: Microsoft tops Wall Street expectations, reports accelerating Azure growth and $37B AI run rate
chunks.chunk_text:
Microsoft tops Wall Street expectations, reports accelerating Azure growth and $37B AI run rate

![Image 7](https://cdn.geekwire.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/msft-capex.png)
**Copilot:**For the quarter, Microsoft 365 Copilot now exceeds 20 million paid seats, up from 15 million in January. That means about 4.4% of the company’s commercial base is on its paid enterprise AI plan.

**Cloud overall:** Microsoft Cloud revenue, which includes Azure, commercial Microsoft 365, LinkedIn, and Dynamics 365, rose 29% to $54.5 billion. The company’s remaining performance obligations, a measure of contracted future revenue, was $627 billion, with a significant part of that backlog tied to OpenAI.

Elsewhere in Microsoft’s business:

*   **Revenue in the More Personal Computing segment**fell 1% to $13.2 billion, with Xbox content and services revenue down 5% and Windows OEM and devices revenue down 2%. Search advertising revenue grew 12%.
*   **The Productivity and Business Processes segment,** which includes Microsoft 365, LinkedIn, and Dynamics 365, grew 17% to $35 billion. LinkedIn revenue rose 12%, and Dynamics 365 revenue increased 22%.
*   **The Intelligent Cloud segment,** home to Azure, grew 30% to $34.7 billion, making it nearly equal in size to the productivity segment for the first time.

chunks.title: MLQ.ai | AI for investors
chunks.chunk_text: Microsoft reported third-quarter earnings that exceeded Wall Street expectations across all major metrics, with the company's cloud and AI businesses driving substantial revenue growth. The Intelligent Cloud segment, home to Azure, posted $34.7 billion in revenue with 30% year-over-year growth, while the company disclosed that its AI business has reached a $37 billion annual revenue run rate, up 123% from a year ago[1](https://mlq.ai/news/microsoft-cloud-and-ai-acceleration-drive-market-outperformance-despite-capital-intensity-concerns/# "Click to view source").

chunks.title: Infographic: Cloud Business Continues to Drive Microsoft's Growth
chunks.heading: Cloud Business Continues to Drive Microsoft's Growth
chunks.chunk_text:
Cloud Business Continues to Drive Microsoft's Growth

Microsoft's Intelligent Cloud segment alone generates more than $100 billion in annual revenue now, as global demand for cloud infrastructure continues to grow. The transition to AI has given another boost to the [global cloud market](https://www.statista.com/chart/34022/cloud-infrastructure-service-revenues/), re-accelerating a market that has grown from less than $100 billion in 2019 to more than $400 billion in 2025. According to estimates from [Synergy Research](https://www.statista.com/chart/18819/worldwide-market-share-of-leading-cloud-infrastructure-service-providers/), Microsoft is one of the market leaders in the cloud infrastructure market, trailing only Amazon Web Services (AWS) in terms of market share.

![Image 15](https://cdn.statcdn.com/contactPerson/367.jpg)

Felix Richter

Data Journalist

[felix.richter@statista.com](mailto:felix.richter@statista.com) +49 (40) 284 841 557

![Image 16: Infographic: Cloud Business Continues to Drive Microsoft's Growth | Statista](https://cdn.statcdn.com/Infographic/images/normal/29863.jpeg)

Description

This chart shows Microsoft's cloud revenue and total revenue per fiscal year.

[Download Chart](https://cdn.statcdn.com/Infographic/images/normal/29863.jpeg "Download Chart")

chunks.chunk_text:
Microsoft tops Wall Street expectations, reports accelerating Azure growth and $37B AI run rate

![Image 7](https://cdn.geekwire.com/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/msft-capex.png)
**Copilot:**For the quarter, Microsoft 365 Copilot now exceeds 20 million paid seats, up from 15 million in January. That means about 4.4% of the company’s commercial base is on its paid enterprise AI plan.

**Cloud overall:** Microsoft Cloud revenue, which includes Azure, commercial Microsoft 365, LinkedIn, and Dynamics 365, rose 29% to $54.5 billion. The company’s remaining performance obligations, a measure of contracted future revenue, was $627 billion, with a significant part of that backlog tied to OpenAI.

Elsewhere in Microsoft’s business:

*   **Revenue in the More Personal Computing segment**fell 1% to $13.2 billion, with Xbox content and services revenue down 5% and Windows OEM and devices revenue down 2%. Search advertising revenue grew 12%.
*   **The Productivity and Business Processes segment,** which includes Microsoft 365, LinkedIn, and Dynamics 365, grew 17% to $35 billion. LinkedIn revenue rose 12%, and Dynamics 365 revenue increased 22%.
*   **The Intelligent Cloud segment,** home to Azure, grew 30% to $34.7 billion, making it nearly equal in size to the productivity segment for the first time.

chunks.title: FY24 Q4 - Performance - Investor Relations - Microsoft
chunks.heading: Fiscal Year 2024 Compared with Fiscal Year 2023
chunks.chunk_text:
Fiscal Year 2024 Compared with Fiscal Year 2023

Revenue increased $33.2 billion or 16% driven by growth across each of our segments. Intelligent Cloud revenue increased driven by Azure. Productivity and Business Processes revenue increased driven by Office 365 Commercial. More Personal Computing revenue increased driven by Gaming.

Cost of revenue increased $8.3 billion or 13% driven by growth in Microsoft Cloud and Gaming, offset in part by a decline in Devices.

Gross margin increased $25.0 billion or 17% driven by growth across each of our segments.

• Gross margin percentage increased slightly. Excluding the impact of the change in accounting estimate for the useful lives of our server and network equipment, gross margin percentage increased 2 points driven by improvement in More Personal Computing.

• Microsoft Cloud gross margin percentage decreased slightly to 71%. Excluding the impact of the change in accounting estimate, Microsoft Cloud gross margin percentage increased slightly driven by improvements in Azure and Office 365 Commercial, inclusive of scaling our AI infrastructure, offset in part by sales mix shift to Azure.

Operating expenses increased $4.0 billion or 7% driven by Gaming, with 7 points of growth from the Activision Blizzard acquisition, and investments in cloud engineering, offset in part by the prior year Q2 charge.

Operating income increased $20.9 billion or 24% driven by growth across each of our segments.

_Productivity and Business Processes_

*   Office Commercial products and cloud services revenue increased $5.8 billion or 14%. Office 365 Commercial revenue grew 16% with seat growth of 7%, driven by small and medium business and frontline worker offerings, as well as growth in revenue per user. Office Commercial products revenue declined 16% driven by continued customer shift to cloud offerings.
*   Office Consumer products and cloud services revenue increased $237 million or 4%. Microsoft 365 Consumer subscribers grew 10% to 82.5 million.
*   LinkedIn revenue increased $1.4 billion or 9% driven by growth across all lines of business – Talent Solutions, Premium Subscriptions, Marketing Solutions, and Sales Solutions.
*   Dynamics products and cloud services revenue increased $1.0 billion or 19% driven by Dynamics 365. Dynamics 365 revenue grew 24% driven by growth across all workloads.

chunks.title: Infographic: Cloud Business Continues to Drive Microsoft's Growth
chunks.heading: Cloud Business Continues to Drive Microsoft's Growth
chunks.chunk_text:
Cloud Business Continues to Drive Microsoft's Growth

by[Felix Richter](mailto:felix.richter@statista.com),

Jan 29, 2026

Cloud computing

Even though Microsoft is best known for Windows and its productivity suite Microsoft 365 (formerly known as Microsoft Office), cloud services have been integral to the company's success over the last couple of years. In the quarter that ended December 31, Microsoft's cloud revenue surpassed $50 billion for the first time, accounting for 63 percent of the company's total revenue.

More impressively, though, Microsoft's diverse cloud offerings, most importantly Azure and Microsoft 365 Commercial and Consumer cloud have been responsible for more than 90 percent of the company's revenue growth over the past two years. While revenues from Intelligent Cloud and other cloud services have grown more than 50 percent between Q2 2024 and Q2 2026, revenue from Microsoft's other products and services have only increased 5 percent.

chunks.title: MLQ.ai | AI for investors
chunks.heading: Financial Performance and Earnings Beat
chunks.chunk_text:
Financial Performance and Earnings Beat

Total revenue reached $82.9 billion, beating the $81.4 billion analyst consensus estimate, while earnings per share jumped 23% to $4.27, above the $4.06 expected by Wall Street[1](https://mlq.ai/news/microsoft-cloud-and-ai-acceleration-drive-market-outperformance-despite-capital-intensity-concerns/# "Click to view source"). The Intelligent Cloud segment, which accounts for a growing portion of company revenue, grew 30% to $34.7 billion, making it nearly equal in size to the productivity segment for the first time[1](https://mlq.ai/news/microsoft-cloud-and-ai-acceleration-drive-market-outperformance-despite-capital-intensity-concerns/# "Click to view source"). Microsoft's commercial remaining performance obligations increased 99% to $627 billion, a measure of contracted future revenue with a significant portion tied to OpenAI and other cloud commitments[1](https://mlq.ai/news/microsoft-cloud-and-ai-acceleration-drive-market-outperformance-despite-capital-intensity-concerns/# "Click to view source").

judgment.reasoning: The response correctly provides the three revenue figures and YoY growth rates, matching the ground truth closely. However, it fails to cite the required SEC-MSFT-* chunks for each year, instead using generic citations [3][4][6]. This omission of the expected citation format and the minor truncation in the analysis prevent full marks.
